name: Borger-Odoorn (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the field and the golden charges
the central band and the shield

description: A yellow field crossed by a broad black horizontal band, with a black shield at the centre bearing (in gold) a crosier, a chevron, and a hunebed (megalithic dolmen).
history: Flag of the municipality of Borger-Odoorn in the province of Drenthe. The charges are taken from the municipal coat of arms; the hunebed refers to the prehistoric megalithic tombs of the Borger area, where the largest hunebed in the Netherlands stands.

features.role: a broad black band across a yellow field
a black shield bearing a golden crosier, chevron and hunebed (dolmen)
